title: Non-record: GQA + LZMA + SLOT eval optimization (val_bpb=1.1240)
author: ibarrajo
body:
## Summary

- Same GQA+LZMA stack as Approach I, but with **SLOT** (eval-time delta optimization) instead of TTT
- Best base BPB (1.1330) of all our approaches — GQA+LZMA stack trains well
- SLOT gives -0.009 BPB improvement at eval time

## Results

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| val_bpb (base) | 1.1330 |
| val_bpb (SLOT) | **1.1240** |
| Artifact size | 14.0 MB |
| Step time | 95 ms |
| Total steps | 6,155 |
| Training time | ~585s |

## Key Findings

- SLOT eval-time delta optimization provides consistent -0.009 BPB improvement
- GQA base model (1.1330) is the strongest base of all our approaches
- But full-attention + TTT still wins overall (B6: 1.1179 vs this 1.1240)
- SLOT is a viable alternative to TTT when attention capacity is limited

## Why Non-record

Despite having the best base BPB, GQA's weaker attention means eval-time techniques (SLOT or TTT) can't close the gap to full-attention + TTT approaches.

## Rule Compliance

- [x] Training < 600s on 8xH100
- [x] Artifact < 16,000,000 bytes
- [x] No val tokens in artifact
- [x] GPTQ calibration within training budget
- [x] SLOT is eval-time only (no training data leakage)
